title: Ficus phaeopoda
taxonomy: Ficus phaeopoda is a species of fig tree in the genus Ficus. It belongs to the family Moraceae, which comprises about 40 genera of trees and shrubs. The Moraceae family is part of the order Rosales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily woody. Ficus phaeopoda is further classified as a member of the order Rosales, the class Magnoliopsida, and the kingdom Plantae. The specific classification of Ficus phaeopoda is a result of its shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species within the Moraceae family.
